author: @Plus3Happiness
text: that's a lot of words to say that trying to fix another is an attempt at fixing the solver's aversion According to psychology, the urge to immediately fix a partner’s or friend’s distress instead of just sitting with them in it isn't empathy; it is your own low frustration tolerance. When someone you love is hurting, and you instantly jump into problem-solving mode, offering x.com/sunnkssdseraph…

author: @Plus3Happiness
text: 'recreating' is a bit imprecise, the sensations are closer to 'stored' and 'reaccessed' when the grooves of conditioning keep getting used, it's much easier to go thru the same process of the body pointing us to something unfelt and the same pattern of resisting it showing up As hard for me to understand as it is, it really does seem like the emotions we most avoid are the ones we unconsciously keep recreating Joe Hudson calls this the “golden algorithm”. Does anyone have a nice story as to why this makes sense?

author: @Plus3Happiness

You were arguing against points I didn’t make and attributing motivations to me that I don’t have. So it seemed appropriate

Most westerners treat the goal of Buddhism as something like gaining maximal freedom to pursue their genetically and culturally pre-programmed goals. They x.com/Plus3Happiness…

author: @Plus3Happiness
text: speaking of Sedona, after dropping reactivity + memory-clearing w/ EMDR, i cant seem to get much from most practices that deal with Emotions (sensations + taking label as real) vs simply sensations Lester Levenson's stuff as well any of you that keep making headway with them? Yesterday someone described it from a Sedona practitioner they had heard it from as opening a door. Things now come and go freely. Whether something comes in or something goes isn't up to you, but the block is gone.

author: @Plus3Happiness

author: @Plus3Happiness

text: oh btw, reactivity-work is simply a meditation or contemplative practice on the nature and experience of emotions unsure why i never framed it like that before, but alas procrastination is a sub-type of reactivity (resistance to felt emotions). reactivity, as a category, cant be one-shotted (canonically) one isnt resisting the situation itself, they're resisting the sensations arising from it either via narrative (thought) or distractions x.com/chrislakin/sta…

The divide in outcomes is the worst I've ever seen. Over the last 5yrs, a group of ~10k people - employees at Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, Nvidia, Meta TBD, founders - have hit retirement wealth of well above $20M (back of the envelope

author: @Plus3Happiness
text: the default-mode network of your brain is to increasingly resist current pain while hoping for some ideal reality this creates a decades-long backlog of unprocessed sensations, which leads to the impression of a rosier past but thankfully you can reverse the mind-trend

author: @Plus3Happiness
text: any 'need' becomes disempowering when it's converted into an indirect strategy and it becomes empowering when it's owned as a part of reality and included in the structures you design, as 'want' you frame anything as a need because you had to justify wants to everyone. family, friends, society at large.
